Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: standard Hire to Retire process typically follows post job ad then screen candidates then conduct interviews then make hiring decision then onboard employee then add to payroll then close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 has edges from Post to Screen from Screen to Decide and from Screen to Interview and from Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: because there is no edge from Interview to Decide and both Interview and Decide only depend on Screen the model allows Decide before Interview or independently of Interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: this suggests an anomaly because hiring decisions are normally expected to be made after interviews rather than before them or without them

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the tail of Model 1 from Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close is otherwise aligned with the expected process order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has edges from Post to Screen and from Post to Interview and from Interview to Decide and then to a loop onboarding structure then to an xor payroll structure then to Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: putting Screen and Interview both directly after Post means Interview can occur without waiting for screening which deviates from usual recruitment logic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the onboarding part is a loop with Onboard and skip so onboarding can be repeated or bypassed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: repeating onboarding is not sensible in a normal hire process and skipping onboarding is a serious violation because a hired employee should be onboarded

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the payroll part is an xor between Payroll and skip so payroll can be omitted before closing the case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: allowing a hired employee to be closed without payroll is a fundamental anomaly because payroll enrollment is an essential outcome of successful hiring

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: when comparing severity Model 1 mainly fails to enforce interview before decision while Model 2 allows omission or repetition of core post hiring activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: therefore Model 1 is closer to a normative Hire to Retire process because its anomaly is a sequencing weakness whereas Model 2 contains more severe structural violations
